# Distributed Cache Coordination

HydraCache should grow toward distributed synchronization without becoming a database or a full distributed map.

The future role model is explicit:

- `Local`: no distributed dependencies
- `Client`: local near-cache plus synchronization with members
- `Member`: participates in ownership and serves peer fetches

```{mermaid}
%%| label: fig-cluster-roles
%%| fig-cap: "HydraCache cluster roles."
flowchart LR
  A[Application Node] --> B{Cluster Mode}
  B --> C[Local]
  B --> D[Client]
  B --> E[Member]
  D --> F[Near Cache]
  D --> G[Invalidation Bus]
  E --> H[Owned Keys]
  E --> I[Peer Fetch Service]
```

Phase 2 adds distributed invalidation. Phase 3 adds member/client ownership and distributed fill coordination.

## Source Notes

Use these files when expanding this chapter:

- [Distributed coordination note](../learning/distributed-cache-coordination.md)
- [Groupcache reread](../../../groupcache/GROUPCACHE_HYDRACACHE_REREAD.md)
- [Hazelcast reread](../../../hazelcast/HAZELCAST_HYDRACACHE_REREAD.md)
- [Olric reread](../../../olric/OLRIC_HYDRACACHE_REREAD.md)
- [Coerce-rs reread](../../../coerce-rs/COERCE_RS_HYDRACACHE_REREAD.md)
